Offices of:
Milpitas Sanitary District

CITY OF MILPITAS, CALIFORNIA

455 E. CALAVERAS BOULEVARD – MILPITAS, CALIFORNIA 95035
Telephone: (408) 262-2310

January 29, 1979

FROM THE OFFICE OF: City Clerk

FILE REF:

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Calif. 95804

Dear Sirs:

There is an apparent discrepancy between the filing schedules for local elections shown on page 6 of your 1979 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and Paragraph C. Committee Supporting or Opposing the Qualification of Local Measures on page 7 of the manual.

The discrepancy has to do with the 1st pre-election statement. The schedule states 40 days before the election and Paragraph C. states 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure qualified to qualify, whichever date is earlier. The two may or may not coincide. Which date prevails?

I assume that the filing schedule was drawn up in accordance with Government Code 84200 and that Elections Code Section 11552 is inoperative by the terms of Elections Code Section 11620.

Very truly yours,

[illegible] Smith
W. E. Smith
City Clerk

enc.
cc: City Attorney

P.S.: We anticipate that an initiative measure will be filed on February 13 to qualify for a June 5, 1979 election.
WES

§ 84101. Statement of organization; filing

Initiative process. Roger Jon Diamond,
Peter H. Alfloratto, Patrick J. Marley and
Patricia V. Talbert. (1975) 7 Southwestern
U.L.Rev. 153.

§ 84102. Statement of organization; contents

Each statement of organization required by Section 84101 shall include:
(a) The name, street address and telephone number, if any, of the committee;
(b) The name, street address and telephone number of each person, if any, with
which the committee is affiliated or connected;
(c) The full name, street address and telephone number, if any, of the treasurer
and other principal officers;
(d) The full name and office sought by * * * any candidate and the title
and name of the ballot measure or, if any, of * * * any measure which the committee supports or opposes as its primary activity. A committee which does not support or
oppose one or more candidates or ballot measures as its primary activity shall provide a brief description of its political activities, including whether it supports
or opposes candidates or measures and whether each candidate or measure has
common characteristics such as a political party affiliation;
(e) A statement whether the committee is controlled or controlled, and if it is
controlled, the name of each candidate or committee by which it is controlled with which it is affiliated;
(f) The disposition of surplus funds which will be made in the event of dissolution;
(g) Such other information as shall be required by the rules or regulations of
the Commission consistent with the purposes and provisions of this chapter.

(Amended by Stats.1977, c. 1095, p. —, § 2.)

Exemption from reporting and disclosure
requirements. See 2 Cal.Adm.Code § 1415.

§ 84202.5 Renumbered § 84202 and amended by Stats.1977, c. 1193, p. —, § 5.)

§ 84201. June or November elections in even-numbered years

Each candidate and each committee supporting or opposing a candidate or candidates or supporting or opposing a ballot measure or ballot measures at an election
held on the first Tuesday after the first Monday in June in an even-numbered years
shall file campaign statements not later than 12 days prior to the election, and not later than June 30, respectively.

Each candidate and each committee supporting or opposing a candidate or candidates or supporting or opposing a ballot measure or ballot measures at an election
held on the first Tuesday after the first Monday in November in even-numbered
years shall file campaign statements not later than 12 days prior to the election, and not later than January 31. The closing dates for such statements
shall be April 23, 15 days prior to the election, and June 30, respectively.

(Added by Stats.1977, c. 1193, p. —, § 5.)

Former § 84201 was renumbered as 84202
and amended by Stats.1977, c. 1193, p. —.

§ 84202. County central committee; candidates

Notwithstanding the provisions of Section * * * N-201, a candidate for county
central committee shall file a campaign statement * * * not later than July 31
following the election and shall not be required to file any additional campaign
statements, unless he has received contributions for the election in which case he
shall observe the requirements of Section §4201. The closing date for such a statement shall be June 30.

This section is not applicable to a committee supporting or opposing one or more
candidates for county central committee, and such committee shall observe the
requirements of Section * * * §4201.

(Formerly § 84200.5, added by Stats.1977, c. 915, p. 2021, § 3, eff. Sept. 20, 1975.

§ 11552. Campaign statements for proponents of measures;
times

(a) Not later than 35 days after a measure has been qualified
for the ballot, the proponent or proponents shall file a campaign
statement, the closing date of which shall be the 28th day following
the qualification of the measure.

(b) If any proposed measure does not qualify for the ballot, the
proponent or proponents shall file a campaign statement within 35
days after the final deadline for circulating the petition, the closing
date of which shall be the 28th day following the deadline.

(Added by Stats. 1973, c. 1186, p. 2479 § 2)

sections.question: There is an apparent discrepancy between the filing schedules for local elections shown on page 6 of your 1979 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and Paragraph C. Committees Supporting or Opposing the Qualification of Local Measures on page 7 of the manual. The discrepancy has to do with the 1st pre-election statement. The schedule states 40 days before the election and Paragraph C. states 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure qualified or failed to qualify, whichever date is earlier. The two may or may not coincide. Which date prevails?
sections.question_synthetic: The City Clerk seeks clarification on conflicting filing deadlines for pre-election campaign statements in the FPPC's 1979 Information Manual: one provision requires filing 40 days before the election, while another requires filing 35 days after measure qualification. The inquiry asks which deadline controls.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section present. The document includes statutory references and background material that appear to be attachments or supporting documents. Confidence is high for classification but there is no response/advice content to extract.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: There is an apparent discrepancy between the filing schedules for local elections shown on page 6 of your 1979 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and Paragraph C. Committees Supporting or Opposing the Qualification of Local Measures on page 7 of the manual. The discrepancy has to do with the 1st pre-election statement. The schedule states 40 days before the election and Paragraph C. states 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure qualified or failed to qualify, whichever date is earlier. The two may or may not coincide. Which date prevails?
embedding.first_500_words: **City of Milpitas, California** 455 E. Calaveras Boulevard — Milpitas, California 95035 Telephone: (408) 262-2310 January 29, 1979 FROM THE OFFICE OF: City Clerk FILE REF: Fair Political Practices Commission Technical Assistance and Analysis Division P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, Calif. 95804 Dear Sirs: There is an apparent discrepancy between the filing schedules for local elections shown on page 6 of your 1979 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and Paragraph C. Committees Supporting or Opposing the Qualification of Local Measures on page 7 of the manual. The discrepancy has to do with the 1st pre-election statement. The schedule states 40 days before the election and Paragraph C. states 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure qualified or failed to qualify, whichever date is earlier. The two may or may not coincide. Which date prevails? I assume that the filing schedule was drawn up in accordance with Government Code 84200 and that Elections Code Section 11552 is inoperative by the terms of Elections Code Section 11620. Very truly yours, W.E. Smith City Clerk enc. cc: City Attorney PS.: We anticipate that an initiative measure will be filed on February 13 to qualify for a June 5, 1979 election. embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of Milpitas City Clerk to the FPPC requesting clarification on a discrepancy between two different pre-election statement filing deadlines in the FPPC's 1979 campaign disclosure manual regarding local ballot measures.
